w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh wp14

w:r.w:t: represents the fundamental business entities of the
It provides a conceptual view of the system structure before transforming the design into a database schema.
The system domain is divided into the following logical areas:
This separation improves clarity and reflects the major functional components of the platform.
The Platform Domain manages the platform users and the stores created within the system.
Represents a platform user who owns or manages a store.
A User can own/manage one or more Stores
Represents an individual online store created on the platform.
A Store is owned by a User
Represents the configuration settings of a store.
Represents the domain used to access a store.
A Store can have one or more
Each StoreDomain belongs to a Store
This domain manages the visual customization and theme configuration of the store.
Represents a predefined theme template available on the platform.
can be used by multiple stores
Represents the theme configuration applied to a specific store.
The Catalog Domain manages the products offered by each store.
Represents a product category used to organize products.
A Store can have multiple Categories
A Category can contain multiple Products
Represents a product offered in a store
A Product belongs to a Store
A Product belongs to a Category
Represents an image associated with a product.
Represents the stock level of a product
The Order Domain manages customer purchases within a store.
Represents a customer who purchases products from a store.
A Store can have multiple Customers
A Customer can place multiple Orders
A Customer can have multiple Addresses
Represents a shipping address for a customer
A Customer can have multiple Addresses
Represents a purchase transaction made by a customer.
An Order belongs to a Store
An Order can have a Payment record
Represents a product included within an order.
This domain manages simulated billing operations used for demonstration purposes in the system.
Represents a simulated payment transaction associated with an order.
Each Payment is associated with one
This domain manages the interaction between the platform and the AI service used for store generation.
Represents a request sent by the user to the AI system.
Represents the configuration generated by the AI to create the initial store structure.
GeneratedStoreConfig is created based on an AIResponse
The AI service generates a structured configuration that represents the initial setup of a new store.
This configuration is returned as a JSON object and is later processed by the system to create the corresponding database entities such as the store, categories, products, and theme configuration.
The generated data typically includes the following elements:
Basic information required to create the store.
Defines the visual style that will be applied to the store.
Defines the initial set of product categories for the store.
Defines the initial set of products generated by the AI system.
Example of Complete AI Generated Configuration
This structured configuration is stored and later used by the platform to automatically create the initial store structure.
Since the system supports multiple stores, all store-related entities contain the attribute:
This ensures proper isolation between tenant data within the shared database.
The presented Domain Model provides a comprehensive conceptual representation of the platform.
It captures the essential entities required to support:
This model forms the foundation for the next phase of the system design, which is the Database Schema Design and ER Diagram development.
